technical.lead: Google Cloud Next '26 (Apr 22-24) launches Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
technical.body: Vertex AI is rebranded and consolidated. Model Garden brings 200+ models (Claude / third-party included), plus Agent Designer, Inbox, Skills, Workspace Studio (a no-code agent builder), Project Mariner (web automation), an A2A protocol production release, and a managed MCP server. A — $750M Agentic AI fund for partners — is announced alongside. — ****

citations.label: SiliconANGLE - Google brings agentic development, optimization, governance under one roof

technical.lead: Tesla Optimus V3 mid-show + Fremont production July-August confirmed by Musk (Apr 22-23)
technical.body: — 37 joints, 1.2 m/s walking gait, repurposed Model S/X line. Initial production is "very slow," with a 1M units/year target. Giga Texas's second factory is under construction, with summer-2027 commissioning. 2026 capex revised up past $25B. Roadster and unsupervised FSD slip.

citations.label: The Driven - Musk delays Roadster, Optimus and unsupervised FSD

technical.body: A 27B Dense (not MoE), Gated DeltaNet + self-attention hybrid, 262K → 1M token context, with a Thinking Preservation mechanism. SWE-bench Verified — 77.2% — , Terminal-Bench 59.3% — beats the prior flagship Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (MoE 397B), reaching the Claude Opus 4.5 / 4.6 line in 27B. BF16 and FP8 builds drop simultaneously on Hugging Face Hub / ModelScope. — ****

citations.label: MarkTechPost - Alibaba Qwen Team Releases Qwen3.6-27B

technical.body: Codex-driven, in research preview for ChatGPT Business / Enterprise / Edu / Teachers. Cross-cuts files / code / tools / Slack / Salesforce, runs cloud-resident inside org permissions. — Free through May 6, then credit-billed. — Positioned as the successor to Custom GPTs, aiming directly at enterprise automation. — ****

citations.label: OpenAI - Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT

technical.lead: Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview hit by unauthorized access on day one (Apr 21-22 reporting)
technical.body: — A Discord group used a contractor's credentials in Anthropic's third-party-vendor environment, combined with the Mercor data leak, and reconstructed the Mythos endpoint URL from Anthropic's naming conventions. They claim they only tried "simple website building," not exploitation. Anthropic says its internal systems are intact.
